message:
harden(handoff): skip symlinks and fall back on malformed YAML in reader

The handoff reader previously failed hard when encountering symlinks in
the handoff directory or when the newest YAML file was malformed/corrupt.
This caused silent failures in multi-agent environments where stale
symlinks or partial writes are common.

Extract discoverHandoffYAMLPaths helper that filters out symlinks (via
Lstat) and non-regular files before returning candidate YAML paths.
FindLatest and ExtractGoalNow now iterate candidates in descending order
and fall back to the next file when the newest is unreadable, rather
than hard-failing. ListSessions tightened to only return sessions with
at least one readable handoff file.

- Add discoverHandoffYAMLPaths with symlink/non-regular file filtering
- FindLatest/ExtractGoalNow: iterate candidates, skip malformed files
- ListSessions: skip empty session directories
- Add comprehensive tests for symlink skipping, YAML fallback, empty dirs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
